deductions.description: slight knee bend in both back handsprings
flexed feet during multiple transitions throughout the routine

deduction_log.narrative: The judge observed slight knee bends during both back handsprings and a noticeable pike in the back tuck, indicating a lack of full body tension. These form breaks accumulated to a 0.20 deduction, impacting the overall cleanliness of the tumbling pass. To improve, the gymnast should focus on actively locking the knees and maintaining a tight hollow body shape throughout the entire flight phase. A judge would note the consistent minor form breaks that prevent this pass from achieving a higher execution score.
deduction_log.injury_signal: This tumbling pass involves significant impact loading on the ankles, knees, and lower back during landings and handsprings. Monitor for any discomfort or pain in these areas, especially with high repetitions. No obvious compensation patterns were observed in this execution, but consistent knee bend can increase joint stress over time.
deduction_log.reason: minor form breaks and landing adjustment

deductions.description: early heel drop before completing the turn
slight wobble at the end of the turn

deduction_log.narrative: The judge noted an early heel drop before the full rotation was completed, which breaks the clean line of the turn. This minor timing error resulted in a 0.05 deduction, indicating a slight loss of control. To refine this, the gymnast should practice holding the relevé until the very last moment of the turn, focusing on a strong finish. A judge would note the slight instability at the end, which suggests a need for stronger core engagement throughout the rotation.
deduction_log.injury_signal: Full turns place rotational stress on the ankles, knees, and hips, especially if balance is compromised. Repetitive turns can lead to overuse injuries if proper alignment and strength are not maintained. No compensation patterns were observed in this execution.

deductions.description: slight knee bend in both back handsprings

deduction_log.narrative: The judge observed slight knee bends during both back handsprings, indicating a lack of full extension and body tension. This form break resulted in a 0.10 deduction, affecting the visual cleanliness of the series. To improve, the gymnast should focus on maintaining a rigid body line and actively pushing through the shoulders to achieve maximum height and extension. A judge would note the consistent knee breaks as a recurring execution fault.
deduction_log.injury_signal: This tumbling pass involves repetitive impact and hyperextension of the wrists, shoulders, and lower back. Consistent knee bend can increase stress on the knee joints during landing. No compensation patterns were observed in this execution, but maintaining a tight body line is crucial for injury prevention.
deduction_log.reason: minor form breaks and landing adjustment

deductions.description: slight arch break in the middle
slight knee bend on second leg through

deduction_log.narrative: The judge noted a slight arch break in the middle of the back walkover, indicating a momentary loss of a tight body line. This minor form break resulted in a 0.05 deduction, affecting the fluidity and extension of the skill. To improve, the gymnast should focus on maintaining a strong core and actively pushing through the shoulders to keep the body straight. A judge would note the slight lack of control through the inverted phase.
deduction_log.injury_signal: Back walkovers place significant demands on wrist flexibility, shoulder stability, and spinal extension. Repetitive arching can contribute to lower back strain if core strength is insufficient. No compensation patterns were observed in this execution.

deductions.description: split not quite 180 degrees, hips slightly open

deduction_log.narrative: The judge observed that the split did not quite reach a full 180-degree angle, with the hips appearing slightly open. This form break resulted in a 0.10 deduction, as full extension is a key requirement for this element. To improve, the gymnast should focus on deep, consistent stretching and active engagement of the hip flexors and extensors to achieve and maintain the full split. A judge would note the visible lack of full 180-degree extension.
deduction_log.injury_signal: Splits require significant flexibility in the hamstrings, hip flexors, and adductors. Overstretching or forcing a split can lead to muscle strains or tears. Consistent, controlled flexibility training is essential. No compensation patterns were observed in this execution.

coaching_summary: This Level 6 Floor routine demonstrates a solid foundation of skills, but consistent minor form breaks and a lack of sustained body tension prevent it from reaching a higher score. The gymnast shows potential, but precision in execution needs to be prioritized. From a judging standpoint, the routine was clean enough to avoid major errors but lacked the crispness and amplitude to be truly competitive.

artistry.notes: The routine's artistry was somewhat flat, lacking consistent expression and dynamic range. Choreography had some repetitive elements, and musicality could be more precise. Chin was down at times.

special_requirements.comment: Performed Round-off Back Handspring Back Tuck and Round-off Back Handspring Back Handspring.
Performed a Split, though with a deduction for not reaching 180 degrees.

top_3_fixes: Focus on maintaining perfectly locked knees and pointed toes throughout all tumbling elements, especially during the flight and rebound phases of back handsprings and the tuck. Implement 'Straight-Leg Rebound Drills' on a tumble track to build muscle memory for a tight body line.

Improve landing control by practicing 'stick it' drills, emphasizing a deep, controlled squat with arms forward to absorb impact without hops or steps. Aim for a stacked Center of Mass over the base of support.

Enhance overall body tension and extension in dance elements and transitions. Actively point toes and engage core muscles even in connecting movements to eliminate flexed feet and improve the aesthetic quality of the routine.
